title: Activate visited button, add restaurant detail page with ratings and notes, and add three restaurants to database

requirements.summary: Update the 'Visited' button in RestaurantCard to turn solid green and display 'Visited' text when clicked, persisting the visited status
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the 'Mark as Visited' button triggers the useMarkVisited mutation
After successful mutation, the button changes to solid green background
The button text changes from 'Mark as Visited' to 'Visited'
The button state persists across page refreshes using the useIsVisited query

file_operations.description: Replace the current visited checkbox implementation with a button that uses the useMarkVisited mutation hook from useQueries.ts. When clicked, call the mutation with the restaurant name. Update the button styling to show solid green background (bg-green-600) when visited, and update text from 'Mark as Visited' to 'Visited' based on the useIsVisited query result. Use the mutation's isLoading state to disable the button during the request.

requirements.summary: Verify RestaurantDetailPage displays full restaurant information with interactive 5-star rating selector and private notes text input
acceptanceCriteria: Restaurant detail page is accessible by clicking a restaurant card
Page displays complete restaurant information including name, cuisine, description, address, and image
5-star rating component allows user to select a rating from 1 to 5 stars
Selected rating is saved to backend using useSaveRating mutation
Text input field labeled 'My Private Notes' accepts user input
Notes are saved to backend using useSaveNote mutation
Previously saved ratings and notes are loaded on page mount using useGetRating and useGetNote queries

file_operations.description: Verify the existing RestaurantDetailPage implementation includes: (1) A 5-star interactive rating selector using the useSaveRating mutation and useGetRating query hooks from useQueries.ts, allowing users to select 1-5 stars with visual feedback showing the current selection; (2) A text input field labeled 'My Private Notes' that uses the useSaveNote mutation and useGetNote query hooks to save and load user notes; (3) Full restaurant information display including name, cuisine, description, address, and image; (4) Proper loading states while fetching restaurant details, ratings, and notes; (5) Authentication checks to ensure only logged-in users can rate and add notes. If any of these elements are missing or incomplete, add or update them accordingly while preserving the Western Colorado rustic aesthetic.
